sections.paragraph: (“a place abounding with gold,” compare Ch. דִּי No. 1), [Dizahab], pr.n. of a place in the desert of Sinai, so called apparently from its abundance of gold, Deu 1:1. I have no doubt but that it is the same place as that now called Dehab on the western shore of the Ælanitic gulf, where there are many palms; see Burckhardt’s Travels in Syria, p. 847, and 1075, my edit., LXX. Καταχρύσεα; compare Euseb. and Jerome in Onom. on this word.
